name: Croquiqnolles
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large mixing bowl, blend the sugar and water; stir in nutmeg.
2. Beat in eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition.
3. Gradually blend in enough flour to make a stiff dough that can be easily handled.
4. Turn out onto lightly floured surface; divide into half.
5. Roll out each half 1/2-inch thick.
6. With a floured 2 1/2-inch doughnut cutter, cut out doughnuts.
7. Heat oil to 375°F.; deep-fry doughnuts until golden brown, turning once.
8. Drain thoroughly on paper towels.
9. Dust with sugar if desired.
NOTE: New Orleans is known for its fried cakes. The name derives from the French verb "croquer," meaning crunch.
